title: pandanus tectorius var. chamissonis
intro: Pandanus tectorius var. chamissonis, also known as the Hawaiian pandanus or Niho Palaoa, is a variety of the pandanus tectorius plant that is native to tropical Pacific islands, particularly Hawaii. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce swelling, and alleviate pain. The health benefits of pandanus tectorius var. chamissonis include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, such as salves and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
